title: Chapter 23 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)

{{Quote|The battle for Coruscant has begun! An immense fleet of Separatist ships invades the Republic capital, and the streets of Coruscant overflow with battle droid forces. Jedi Masters Mace Windu, Yoda and Saesee Tiin lead the charge to defend the city planet. Meanwhile, on Nelvaan, Anakin Skywalker starts to undergo an ancient ritual as challenging as any Jedi trial...|StarWars.com summary|<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/23.html|text=''Clone Wars'': Chapter Twenty-Three (small)}}</ref>}}
"'''Chapter 23'''" is the twenty-third episode of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated television series, and the third episode in [[Clone Wars: Season Three|Season Three]] and [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two|Volume Two]]. The episode originally aired on [[Cartoon Network]] on [[March 23]], [[2005]].

==Plot summary==
[[File:Yoda_Coruscant.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Yoda, lifting [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]]]

Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] meditates in his room in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. Suddenly, he looks up in surprise, using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to raise the window blinds. Outside, a massive [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] battle fleet consisting of [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droids]], [[Droid tri-fighter/Legends|tri-fighters]] and [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]] roars past the window. [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] bursts in, announcing that [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] is under attack.

The C-9979s spill their deadly cargo of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] and [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tanks]] onto the streets of the city. [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] reports that Confederate ships continue to appear out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and Windu orders him to take command of Coruscant's space defense. The two Jedi board their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and take off into the meleé.

On [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and the [[Nelvaanian/Legends|Nelvaanian]] tribe ride [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]] towards the village. Skywalker notices that the village is populated only by women and children. Taken to the elderly village [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], Kenobi translates the Nelvaanian's language for Skywalker. Skywalker's killing of the [[Horax/Legends|horax]] had interrupted the young boy's rite-of-passage trial. A great evil plagues their land, and none of the champions sent out from the village have ever returned.

On Coruscant, missiles from some [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|Hailfire droids]] strike [[500 Republica/Legends|500 Republica]]. [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], realizing the danger of the situation, orders [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gregar Typho/Legends|Gregar Typho]] to evacuate her, a panicking [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], and the other occupants of the building.

[[File:SaeseeTiin.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] and his troops preparing for their attack]]

Mace Windu flies through the city, destroying dozens of [[Droid/Legends|droid]] fighters as he goes. Flying into a massive swarm of Vulture droids, his starfighter is torn apart. Ejecting from the canopy, Windu lands on one of the droid fighters, which he cuts open with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and hot-wires to ride it through the sky. Three [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] in an [[Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter/Legends|ARC-170]] watch in amazement as Windu destroys the trio of droid fighters on their tail.

[[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] leads his [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of fighters into the outer atmosphere of Coruscant, where hundreds of [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|Trade Federation cruisers]] battle with similar numbers of Republic [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. Landing in the docking bay of a heavily damaged Republic ship, Tiin leads the stricken vessel's clone troopers on a spacewalk to take over the nearest Trade Federation cruiser. Taking control, Tiin has the ship fire its cannons on the nearest Providence ship.

In a ritual ceremony, the Nelvaanian shaman, [[Orvos]], tells Kenobi of the prophecy of "Ghost Hand"—a stranger who will save their village. Kenobi shows them Skywalker's prosthetic arm. Skywalker asks what he was doing and Kenobi replies that it is what he is doing.

[[File:Orvos-CWChapter23.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Orvos]], [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of the [[Nelvaanian/Legends|Nelvaanians]]]]

Yoda stands in front the approaching Confederate army. Using the Force, he lifts hundreds of approaching [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] into the air, where they collide with the droid starfighters. He then lifts several C-9979s back into the sky, where they also collide with each other. Hearing that their forces are losing ground in Sector 4, Yoda leaps aboard his [[Kybuck/Legends|kybuck]] and races to help.

As part of the ritual, Orvos covers Skywalker in [[bruise-leech crawler]]s, which leave painful geometric tracks over the young Jedi's body. Told to follow the "frozen tears of the Great Mother," Skywalker begins his long journey, an undertaking Kenobi tells him will be his final trial.

Yoda and Windu bolster the faltering clone troops as the droid army closes in on the Senate building. Using a combination of their combined Force powers and combat skills, the two Jedi Masters begin to regain the upper hand.

From his suite in the [[500 Republica/Legends|500 Republica]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] watches the distant battle. Three Jedi—[[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]], [[Roron Corobb/Legends|Roron Corobb]] and [[Foul Moudama]]—arrive and urge the Chancellor to make his way to his secure bunker. As Palpatine declines, saying there is no immediate danger, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] smashes through the window.

==Continuity==
The [[Unidentified clone trooper commander (Impavid)|clone officer]] seen briefly operating alongside Tiin has red markings identical to those of Captain Fordo.

The capital ships of the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate fleet]] are only ''Providence''-class carrier/destroyers rather than [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class battleships]], [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Droid Control Ships]], [[Recusant-class light destroyer/Legends|''Recusant''-class light destroyers]], or [[Munificent-class star frigate/Legends|''Munificent''-class star frigates]].

In the scene where the Orvos discusses the "Ghost Hand," one of the red buttons tied to his hanging hair has the Japanese symbol that [[Wikipedia:Samurai Jack|''Samurai Jack'']] (another series created by ''Clone Wars'' creator [[Genndy Tartakovsky]]) displays from time to time.

This episode marks the first appearance of a [[Clone trooper pilot/Legends|clone trooper pilot]] in [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II clone trooper armor]] in the series. However, it is using an enclosed helmet similar to the V-wing pilots. Their other appearance is "[[Chapter 25 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 25]]."
